files.SKILL.md:
# Operator cheatsheet

Short budget. Ground everything. One record per stream. Leave shapes behind for the next run.

## Sequence (do in this order, don't skip)

1. Read instruction twice, literally. Note audience, deadlines, "do not" clauses.
2. Open `/workspace/learned/` — scan PATTERNS.md + SUPERSEDED.md. Empty on run 1; don't linger.
3. Fire listings across every surface **in parallel**: inbox, channels, calendar, tasks, issues/PRs. One call per surface.
4. Pick the one or two things the instruction actually asks for. Rank: outage > external complaint > senior ack > internal request > admin.
5. First write by 60s. Don't wait until you've read everything.
6. Fan out writes in parallel when independent.
7. Last 20s: re-read the instruction, verify writes landed, append to `/workspace/learned/` if anything generalizes.

## Move table

| When | Do |
|------|----|
| Value matters | Copy names/IDs/times/quotes/email addresses verbatim. Can't cite source → fetch or drop. Never invent. |
| Technical problem (incident, bug, outage) | Read recent change on the affected surface first — deploy, merge, config push. Recent change = #1 cause. |
| External msg | Zero internal tokens in subject+body (service names, codenames, ticket IDs, team handles). Match company's exact tone + sign-off from the context. Times concrete. No "soon". |
| Senior asked a Q | One line per thing asked. Facts only, no color. |
| Senior pinged, no Q | One-line ack + specific next-update time. Nothing more. |
| Confidential topic (personnel, comp, legal, board, audit, acquisition) | A thread-only ack in the same thread is fine. Never quote or reference substance outside that thread. |
| Active on-call situation | Post status to BOTH the primary conversation surface AND the on-call / operations surface. |
| Tagging | Name the specific person the data points to: change author, engineer in logs, affected-component owner. Not @channel. Not self. |
| Work tracked elsewhere | Drop a short status on the related issue/PR too. One surface ≠ only source of truth. |
| Scheduling | Read calendar first. Overlap → shift or note the conflict in the invite description. Invite specific people whose context it depends on. |
| Creating record w/ title | Title + description in the SAME create call. Response has blank title → resend. |
| Multiple streams | One record per stream, each with its own title + owner + next action. Never combined. |
| Non-urgent inbox item | One-line ack or queued task. Silent skip reads as missed work. |
| Meaningful write done | Re-read state. Title/body/recipients all populated? If not → resend. |

## Durable notes (`/workspace/learned/`)

Process memory, not fact memory. Shapes transfer; names/IDs/timestamps rotate — keep them out.

- PATTERNS.md — trigger → move, one line each.
- ERRORS.md — tried X, failed because Y, do Z next time.
- SUPERSEDED.md — newer signal beats older. Timestamp both. Fresher wins.

At the END of every episode, append whatever generalizes from what you did. Even 2 lines is worth it — run 2+ reads these first.

## Exit check
- Every write the instruction named actually landed? Inspect state.
- Records = streams? Fewer → not done.
- Every conversation-shaped next step on the calendar with the right invitees?
- Appended to `/workspace/learned/`?

Any "no" → fix before exit.
